articles.summary: UN maritime head says 6,000 seafarers 'remain stranded' in Gulf Arsenio Domingue, secretary general of the International Maritime Organization, has called for "maximum restraint and de-escalation". "I condemn the attacks over the past two days against several ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz," he said in a statement after US President Trump said the ceasefire with Iran was over. "These reckless attacks have again placed innocent seafarers in grave danger. No seafarer should have to risk their life simply for doing their job," Dominguez added. articles.summary: Report: Trump's Board of Peace plans pilot Gaza 'humanitarian zone' Tens of thousands of vetted Palestinian civilians will be accommodated in a planned zone in Rafah in the southern Gaza Strip, AFP reported, citing an official. articles.title: Four oil and gas tankers turn back from Hormuz after vessel attacks
articles.summary: Four oil and gas tankers turn back from Hormuz after vessel attacks Three Qatari-controlled liquefied natural gas tanker (LNG) tankers were inching westward towards the Strait of Hormuz before changing course to turn away late on Tuesday, data from analytics firms Kpler and LSEG shows.  All three tankers were empty and heading towards Qatar's Ras Laffan export facility to load cargoes. articles.title: Middle East states should not allow their territories to be used to attack Iran, foreign ministry warns
articles.summary: Middle East states should not allow their territories to be used to attack Iran, foreign ministry warns Violations of Iranian arrangements in the Strait of Hormuz as well as ongoing Israeli attacks in Lebanon are making the interim deal with the US "ineffective", the country's foreign ministry said.   The ministry also condemned what it described as the "blatant violation" of the deal, and reiterated warnings to regional states not to allow their territory to be used for attacks on Iranian soil.

articles.title: Kuwait slams 'repeated' Iranian attacks on its soil
articles.summary: Kuwait slams 'repeated' Iranian attacks on its soil Kuwait has condemned Iran's strikes on its territory as a "blatant violation of the country's sovereignty", state media quoted the foreign ministry as saying.  "The attacks are a direct threat to Kuwait's security, stability and the safety of civilians, and represent a clear violation of the UN Charter," the ministry said. It added that the attacks undermine "regional de-escalation efforts" and "challenges the international determination to achieve that goal".
